subject: LU-10589 tests: Log nested tests differently

messages.message: Uploaded patch set 1.

Patch Set 1:

Looks good to me.

Patch Set 1:

This patch is more to show nature of the problem with nested tests seen in LU-10589 and offer one potential solution.

Not sure if this is how we want to go forward.

Patch Set 1:

Code-wise looks good to me.

Patch Set 1: Verified+1

Build Successful 

https://build.whamcloud.com/job/lustre-reviews-patchless/3316/ : SUCCESS

https://build.whamcloud.com/job/lustre-reviews/61650/ : SUCCESS

Patch Set 1:

Onyx Autotest started enforced custom session. The following test parameters were specified: testlist=sanity-dom fortestonly=true.

Patch Set 1:

Failed enforced test custom on CentOS 7.0/x86_64 uploaded by Onyx Autotest from onyx-30vm5: https://testing.whamcloud.com/test_sessions/4a30fbc7-a755-4127-b75c-047d3fb5ee23 ran 3 tests.  1 tests failed: sanity-dom.

Patch Set 1:

So this look like what I intended/expected:
https://testing.whamcloud.com/test_sets/3e0ca08e-296d-11e9-b97f-52540065bddc

Just have to decide if that's what we want.

Patch Set 1:

Though I note that there appears to be no logs pulled out for the sanity & sanityn tests.  Not sure what's up there or what to do about it (or if it's a result of how I failed them).

Patch Set 1: Code-Review+1

(1 comment)

I agree that this is one way to fix the issue. I think this is an improvement over what exists today; it removed the false fail of the last test in a 'nested' test suite. So, that's a big improvement.

Patch Set 1: -Code-Review

I should be careful with a +1 here.

Patch Set 3: Code-Review-1

Something's not right with this change.

Before this patch logs say:

 [  111.564930] Lustre: DEBUG MARKER: == sanity-dom test sanity: Run sanity with Data-on-MDT files ========================================= 04:37:52 (1551519472)

After this patch logs say:

 [  101.232857] Lustre: DEBUG MARKER: == sanity-dom test sanity: Run ======================================================================= 17:09:12 (1551564552)

I am not sure what was the other effects you were hoping for, but I still see test output split into sanity/sanityn.

See my testruns here (with the patch): http://testing.linuxhacker.ru:3333/lustre-reports/128/testresults/sanity-dom-ldiskfs-DNE-centos7_x86_64-centos7_x86_64/

and here without for comparison: http://testing.linuxhacker.ru:3333/lustre-reports/125/testresults/sanity-dom-ldiskfs-DNE-centos7_x86_64-centos7_x86_64/

Patch Set 3:

Patrick, could you please refresh this so that we can avoid the spurious test failures.

Patch Set 3:

Hmm, yeah.

In Vancouver, Oleg said he talked to James and James would be taking this one over to sort it out.  I didn't confirm this with James...? :)

parents.subject: LU-12560 tests: Use full path for test-groups

commit.subject: LU-10589 tests: Log nested tests differently
commit.message:
LU-10589 tests: Log nested tests differently

sanity-dom runs select tests from other Lustre test suites.
Currently, the start/end logging for the
"outer" test ends up split by the "inner" tests.

Change logging to not do that so failures are associated
with correct test(s).

Signed-off-by: Patrick Farrell <pfarrell@whamcloud.com>
Signed-off-by: James Nunez <jnunez@whamcloud.com>
Change-Id: I31b8194039c46b1db6b4752c425e72a4bd124a27
